chunk_summary: The ICDS Anganwadi Service Package provides six linked services, including supplementary nutrition, to address pediatric malnutrition. Clinical linkage involves eligibility confirmation, service delivery, and escalation decisions based on nutritional risk and anthropometry.
learning_objectives: Understand the six linked services provided under the ICDS Anganwadi Service Package.
Learn the clinical workflow for managing pediatric malnutrition through Anganwadi centers.
Identify the entitlements for children based on age and nutritional risk under the Supplementary Nutrition Rules.
Recognize the importance of registration and follow-up in improving access to nutrition services.
key_facts: ICDS Anganwadi Services include six components: supplementary nutrition, pre-school non-formal education, nutrition and health education, immunization, health check-up, and referral services.
Children aged 6–36 months receive Take Home Ration (THR), while children aged 3–6 years receive morning snacks and a hot cooked meal.
Malnourished children are entitled to higher calorie and protein supplementation under the Supplementary Nutrition Rules (2017).
Growth monitoring, feeding demonstrations, and structured follow-up are critical for addressing early growth faltering.
Escalation pathways include Anganwadi-based follow-up, PHC/CHC assessment, or NRC admission based on anthropometry and nutritional risk.
reference_quote: Supplementary Nutrition Rules specify that children 6–36 months commonly receive THR, children 3–6 years receive morning snacks and a hot cooked meal at the center, and malnourished children receive higher calorie and protein supplementation.
